message:
fix(web): resolve WebKit visibility edge case for sequence grid renderer

Problem:
--------
On WebKit browsers (Safari, iOS Safari), the sequence grid could appear
permanently black/blank when only a small portion of the canvas was visible
in the viewport. This occurred because:

1. The IntersectionObserver was configured with threshold: 0.05 (5%)
2. When scrolling the page such that only a small sliver of the canvas
   was visible (< 5%), the observer callback didn't fire
3. The renderer remained in its paused state from when it was fully hidden
4. Users saw a blank canvas even though part of it was on screen

Root Cause Analysis:
--------------------
The IntersectionObserver threshold of 0.05 was intended to avoid resuming
the renderer for trivially small intersections. However, this created a
dead zone where 0-5% visibility kept the renderer paused indefinitely.

WebKit's intersection calculation appears more conservative than Chrome/
Firefox in some edge cases, making this issue more pronounced on Safari.

Fix:
----
Changed the IntersectionObserver threshold from 0.05 to 0, meaning the
callback fires as soon as *any* pixel of the canvas becomes visible.

This ensures:
- Renderer resumes immediately when any part becomes visible
- No more blank canvas states on partial visibility
- Consistent behavior across all browsers

The minor performance cost of resuming at 0% instead of 5% is negligible
since the actual rendering work is frame-limited anyway.

Also includes trivial whitespace cleanup in CanvasSequenceGridRenderer.
